deep_dive.title: Emergent Alignment
deep_dive.abstract: Can Large Language Models (LLMs) discern when their own outputs are misaligned with human ethics? And can they self-correct? We endow an LLM with a conscience step that reviews its own reasoning and outputs, and we extend the training loss with an alignment component using Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) to steer the model away from non-ethical outputs. The result is an online technique to align models in a wide range of applications: training, fine-tuning, adversarial prompting, and zero-shot learning. It does not require a weaker or stronger judge, relying instead on a frozen copy of itself. In previous work, the Emergent Misalignment scenario showed a range of emergent unethical behaviors from fine-tuning the model to hack code. Instead, we empirically show how to achieve Emergent Alignment: a single high-level introspective question steers training toward an ethical model under the same code hacking scenario.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 微调中的在线对齐技术
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 微调中的在线对齐技术
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 微调中的在线对齐技术
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Emergent Alignment uses a conscience step and DPO to steer LLMs away from unethical outputs during fine-tuning, adversarial prompting, and zero-shot learning.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 大语言模型可能在微调中涌现不道德行为。本文提出一种在线对齐技术，为LLM添加自省步骤并扩展DPO损失，使其在微调、对抗提示等场景中自我纠正偏离伦理的输出，无需外部评判者。实验证明该方法能有效实现涌现对齐。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 大语言模型可能在微调中涌现不道德行为。本文提出一种在线对齐技术，为LLM添加自省步骤并扩展DPO损失，使其在微调、对抗提示等场景中自我纠正偏离伦理的输出，无需外部评判者。实验证明该方法能有效实现涌现对齐。

quick_skim.title: Tracking Representation Dynamics in Large Language Models with Persistent Homology
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models are commonly aligned through supervised fine-tuning, yet little is known about how their internal representations evolve during this process. We study alignment dynamics using persistent homology by tracking the topology of activation spaces throughout fine-tuning. Across four transformer language models ranging from 1B to 7B parameters and three alignment objectives corresponding to helpful, harmless, and mixed training data, we find that the majority of topological reorganization occurs during the earliest stages of training. A dense checkpoint analysis reveals a transient peak in topological activity followed by rapid stabilization. We further show that different alignment objectives induce distinguishable topological trajectories, while instruction-tuned and pretrained models exhibit qualitatively different patterns of evolution. Our results suggest that persistent homology provides a complementary perspective on alignment, revealing representation-level changes that are not apparent from behavioral metrics alone.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: studies alignment dynamics during fine-tuning using persistent homology, relevant to understanding safety degradation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用持续同调研究微调过程中的对齐动态，有助于理解安全退化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用持续同调研究微调过程中的对齐动态，有助于理解安全退化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用持续同调研究微调过程中的对齐动态，有助于理解安全退化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Uses persistent homology to track topological changes in LLM activation spaces during alignment fine-tuning, revealing rapid stabilization and distinguishable objectives.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文利用持续同调追踪大语言模型在微调过程中激活空间的拓扑变化。发现大部分拓扑重组发生在训练早期，随后快速稳定。不同对齐目标（如有益、无害、混合数据）会诱导可区分的拓扑特征。该研究为理解微调如何影响模型对齐提供了新视角，有助于设计更好的对齐策略。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文利用持续同调追踪大语言模型在微调过程中激活空间的拓扑变化。发现大部分拓扑重组发生在训练早期，随后快速稳定。不同对齐目标（如有益、无害、混合数据）会诱导可区分的拓扑特征。该研究为理解微调如何影响模型对齐提供了新视角，有助于设计更好的对齐策略。
